title: Add interoperability verification coverage and workflow alignment documentation
summary: Add verification coverage and release-facing documentation for v0.5.0 so the documented client/server workflow can be tested and explained as one coherent operating model. The work should confirm that workflow-critical rules, generated scaffolding, lifecycle semantics, commit gating, file-intent ergonomics, bootstrap normalization, version-surface clarity, and ticket-related expectations are either enforced consistently or documented precisely where protocol support ends and convention begins.
outputs: Verification coverage for workflow-critical client/server interoperability behavior
Documentation that explains the supported lifecycle and ticket-workflow contract for v0.5.0
Evidence that mandatory workflow requirements are either server-supported or explicitly documented as convention
Release-facing explanation of how rules, generated bootstrap behavior, and runtime semantics align for the supported v0.5.0 workflow
acceptance_criteria: Tests or equivalent verification checks cover workflow-critical alignment between repository rules and generated initialization rules for the sections treated as protocol-critical in v0.5.0.
Verification coverage confirms the documented canonical lifecycle model for start, progress/update, verify, commit, and terminal completion as exposed to clients.
Verification coverage confirms commit is blocked when verify has not passed and when there are no changes to commit.
Verification coverage confirms any supported ergonomic file-intent helper workflow preserves canonical ordering and validation guarantees.

Verification coverage confirms bootstrap-generated state and runtime-expected state remain aligned closely enough for predictable client reasoning, or documentation explicitly states the intended normalized differences.
Verification coverage or release-facing documentation confirms version surfaces remain clear and non-misleading across release, server/package, and schema contexts.
Verification coverage confirms clients can complete all mandatory supported v0.5.0 workflow steps using server-supported primitives without relying on undocumented behavior.
If ticket persistence remains mandatory protocol in v0.5.0, verification coverage confirms the corresponding server-supported workflow and synchronization expectations.
If ticket persistence is intentionally treated as convention rather than protocol, the documentation states that boundary explicitly and consistently.
Release-facing documentation explains what is enforced by the server, what is provided as helper behavior, and what remains client-side operating convention.
Repository verification passes with the new interoperability coverage and documentation updates in place.
tasks: Review the final v0.5.0 plan and completed implementation tickets to identify which workflow requirements are protocol-enforced versus documented convention.
Add or extend verification coverage for workflow-critical alignment between repository rules, generated initialization rules, and runtime lifecycle behavior.
Add or extend verification coverage for explicit commit gating on verified state and presence of changes to commit.
Add or extend verification coverage for the supported client-facing lifecycle path so the documented workflow is backed by executable checks where feasible.
Add or extend verification coverage for any supported ergonomic file-intent helper workflow and its invalid-ordering rejection behavior.
Add or extend verification coverage or contract checks for normalized bootstrap state expectations and version-surface clarity where those are part of the supported v0.5.0 contract.
Document the final v0.5.0 interoperability model, including lifecycle semantics, ticket expectations, commit policy, bootstrap/version consistency, and any protocol-versus-convention boundary.
Update release-facing docs so operators and client implementers can understand which behaviors are guaranteed by the server and which remain external workflow discipline.
Run repository verification and capture concise evidence that interoperability coverage and documentation remain consistent with the implemented workflow.
notes: This ticket closes the loop on v0.5.0 by proving and documenting the final client/server contract rather than introducing another major workflow redesign.

Keep the focus on interoperability-critical behavior described in docs/draft_0.5.0.md: rule alignment, lifecycle clarity, commit gating, file-intent ergonomics, ticket-protocol boundary clarity, bootstrap normalization, and version-surface coherence.
Prefer concise verification evidence and release-facing explanation over large internal narrative dumps.
If some behaviors cannot be covered by automated checks directly, document the manual verification rationale and the exact boundary of support.
The goal of this ticket is to ensure the documented workflow and the implemented workflow are observably the same thing for the supported v0.5.0 surface.
